title: Lucas Learning
content:
{{Top|rwc|notoc=1}}
{{RealCompany
|image=[[File:Learninglogo.svg]]
|name=Lucas Learning
|founded=[[1996]]
|founder=[[George Lucas]]
|dissolved=[[2001]]
|headquarters=[[Wikipedia:San Rafael, California|San Rafael]], [[Wikipedia:United States|US]]
|parent=
|subsid=
|website=[http://www.lucaslearning.com lucaslearning.com]
}}
'''Lucas Learning''' was a subsidiary of [[LucasArts]] in order to provide challenging, engaging and fun educational software for classrooms.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucaslearning.com/aboutus/about_mission.htm|text=Lucas Learning - Company Mission|date=2001-12-20|archivedate=20011220052520}}</ref> They have created many award-winning educational games for the [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] such as [[Star Wars Episode I: The Gungan Frontier|''The Gungan Frontier'']] and [[Star Wars: DroidWorks|''DroidWorks'']] were based on the national curriculum.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucaslearning.com/aboutus/about_letter.htm|text=Lucas Learning - Letter to Educators|date=2002-02-08|archivedate=20020208041204}}</ref>

The company was located in San Rafael, California,<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucaslearning.com/aboutus/about_george.htm|text=Lucas Learning - George Lucas' Vision|date=2002-02-08|archivedate=20020208123642}}</ref> and was headed by former MECC senior vice president of development and creative director Susan Schilling.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-62625345.html|text=MECC executive to join Lucas firm|author=Alexander, Steve|date=1996-02-24|archivedate=20170221010100}}</ref> Shilling asserted that Lucas was personally involved with the products and that a company mantra was to stay away from violence.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.highbeam.com/doc/1G1-122189613.html|text=Game developers dazzle E3 crowds with new titles and technology|author=Olson, Karen Torme|date=2000-05-17|archivedate=20170221010348}}</ref> They released games from 1998 until announcing their cancellation of a Mac version of [[Star Wars: Super Bombad Racing|''Star Wars Super Bombad Racing'']] in mid 2001, the year Lucas Learning was eventually reorganized into '''Edutopia''', or the '''George Lucas Educational Foundation''', independent from [[Lucasfilm]] and left the market.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.macworld.com/article/1018073/bombad.html|text=Lucas Learning axes Mac game, exits consumer market|date=2001-06-20|archivedate=20190705141523}}</ref>

{{Company-stub|real}}
